name: Eta v2 watchlist: drive composite score down without breaking allocation invariants

description: Baseline at v2 ship head — watchlist score 2.95, fail_catch 6.29M minors, bind/pure/retry zero-allocation invariants intact

asi.hypothesis: establish post-merge watchlist baseline before optimization iterations
main_optimization_targets: pure_reused_rt_min_ns (currently below timer floor in this run; mean ~2us)
asi.next_action_hint: profile fail_catch hotpath in effect_direct.ml: catch creates a fresh Typed_fail.key per invocation and copies the frame record. 6.29M minor / 100k iters = 62.9 words per fail/catch round trip - too high. v1 baseline of 1.05M = 10.5 words per round trip suggests Cause.Fail allocation is unavoidable but the surrounding scaffolding doubles or triples it.

description: Mutable fail_key ref to eliminate frame copy + with_binding in catch — no allocation savings but broke Observability.statuses test

asi.hypothesis: make fail_key a mutable ref in frame to eliminate frame record copy and inner with_binding per catch iteration
asi.result: zero allocation savings (6291444 vs 6291435 baseline) — frame copy is not the bottleneck. Observable evidence: runtime chain rebuilding dominates (handler→go→Effect.catch allocates ~17 words/iter). Breakage: Observability.statuses test expects catch to use default_renderer for typed failures inside catch scope.
asi.root_cause: The fail_catch 'prebuilt' variant is NOT fully prebuilt. Only the outermost catch is prebuilt; the handler `fun `Boom -> go (i-1) (acc+1)` calls `go` at runtime which allocates new catch/fail/handler closures each iteration. The chain-rebuilding cost (~17-20 words/iter) dominates the frame operations.
asi.next_action: Prebuild the entire fail_catch chain at construction time (like bind chain is fully prebuilt). Use tail-recursive outward build: start with pure n as leaf, then wrap catches outward. Each handler is `fun _ -> prebuilt_next_node` (no runtime function call). This eliminates chain-rebuilding allocations entirely.
